description: Strata 'modern complete' — close P1 (production-blockers: real SSE-S3, notification publisher, replicator, access log delivery, structured logs, full metrics, health probes, virtual-hosted URLs), P2 (audit log, per-part GET, versioning null literal, website redirects, OTel, admin CLI, race harness), and P3 (SSE-KMS, Inventory, Access Points, ScyllaDB CI, multi-cluster RADOS, shard resize, audit export, examples, protobuf manifest). No legacy, no AWS-niche.

userStories.title: Master-key provider interface + env/file providers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a pluggable Provider abstraction with built-in env-var and file-with-hot-reload backends for the SSE-S3 master key.
acceptanceCriteria: New internal/crypto/master package with Provider interface: Resolve(ctx) (key32 []byte, keyID string, error)
Built-in env provider: STRATA_SSE_MASTER_KEY=<hex64> with optional STRATA_SSE_MASTER_KEY_ID (default 'env-1')
Built-in file provider: STRATA_SSE_MASTER_KEY_FILE=/path watches mtime for hot-reload
Invalid key length (not 32 bytes) is fatal at startup with structured-log error
Unit tests cover both providers, hot-reload, missing config
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/crypto/master/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/crypto/master: Provider interface, EnvProvider (STRATA_SSE_MASTER_KEY), FileProvider (mtime hot-reload), FromEnv selector. ErrNoConfig + ErrInvalidKeyLength sentinels. Caller fatals on error.

userStories.title: Master-key Vault provider (per-cluster role)
userStories.description: As an operator running Vault, I want to wrap the master key via Vault Transit using a per-Strata-cluster role.
acceptanceCriteria: Built-in vault provider: STRATA_SSE_MASTER_KEY_VAULT=<addr>:<transit-path>, plus STRATA_SSE_VAULT_ROLE_ID + STRATA_SSE_VAULT_SECRET_ID
Periodic refresh every 5 minutes; cached key reused between refreshes
Vault unreachable on first init is fatal; on refresh keep last good key with WARN log
Unit tests with httptest mock of Vault Transit endpoints
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/crypto/master/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/crypto/master/vault.go: VaultProvider with AppRole login + Transit export-encryption-key. STRATA_SSE_MASTER_KEY_VAULT=<addr>:<transit-export-path>; key id is 'vault-v<N>' from highest version. Lazy refresh on Resolve when stale (cfg.Refresh, default 5m). Initial fetch fatal; refresh failure logs WARN via injected slog.Logger and reuses last good key. FromEnv prefers vault > file > env.

userStories.title: AES-256-GCM chunk encryption package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a small crypto package that AEAD-encrypts and decrypts a chunk under a per-object DEK with a deterministic IV.
acceptanceCriteria: New internal/crypto/sse package: EncryptChunk(dek, oid, chunkIndex, plaintext) ([]byte, error) and DecryptChunk symmetric
IV derived as HKDF-SHA256(oid, chunkIndex) producing 12 bytes
WrapDEK(masterKey, dek) and UnwrapDEK(masterKey, wrapped) using AES-256-GCM
Unit tests: round-trip; mutated ciphertext rejected; mismatched IV rejected
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/crypto/sse/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/crypto/sse: EncryptChunk/DecryptChunk seal/open AES-256-GCM with deterministic 12-byte IV from HKDF-SHA256(oid||chunkIndexBE, info='strata-sse-chunk-iv'). WrapDEK/UnwrapDEK use AES-256-GCM with random 12-byte nonce prefixed to ciphertext. ErrInvalidKeySize / ErrShortWrappedDEK sentinels. Chunk ciphertext is just AEAD output (no nonce prefix — IV is recomputed on decrypt). 18 tests cover round-trip, mutation rejection (cipher + tag), mismatched oid/chunkIndex, wrong DEK / master, truncated wrapped blob, IV determinism + diversity, deterministic ciphertext for equal inputs.

userStories.title: SSE-S3 wired into PutObject/GetObject path
userStories.description: As a security/compliance team, when I PUT with x-amz-server-side-encryption AES256 the chunk bytes are encrypted at rest.
acceptanceCriteria: Add objects.sse_key (blob) and objects.sse_key_id (text) columns via additive ALTER
PutObject with AES256 generates a per-object DEK, wraps under master key, stores wrapped DEK plus key id, encrypts chunks via internal/crypto/sse
GetObject and HEAD echo x-amz-server-side-encryption AES256 from persisted state and decrypt chunks transparently
Plaintext objects (no header on PUT) continue to work when sse_key column is NULL
Unit tests: round-trip per object; corrupted ciphertext yields 500 InternalError; plaintext interop
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Object grew SSEKey []byte + SSEKeyID string. Cassandra schema additive ALTER objects ADD sse_key blob / sse_key_id text. cmd/strata-gateway/main.go calls master.FromEnv(); ErrNoConfig leaves Master nil and AES256 PUT returns 500. internal/s3api/sse_io.go: sseEncryptingReader emits 4 MiB ciphertext chunks (pChunkSize = 4 MiB - 16 byte tag) so backend chunk index == crypto chunk index. sseDecryptingReader is range-aware in plaintext space; Preload() eagerly decrypts the first chunk so AEAD failures yield 500 before the response status is committed. ETag persisted on the object row is plaintext-MD5; manifest ETag/Size remain ciphertext. OID for DeriveChunkIV is the object key (deterministic per object; DEK uniqueness is the actual security boundary). Tests cover small + multi-chunk round-trip, range across crypto-chunk boundary, plaintext interop, AES256 PUT without master configured returning 500, and corrupted-ciphertext yielding non-2xx via memory-backend CorruptFirstChunk helper.

userStories.title: SSE-S3 wired through multipart (Initiate/UploadPart/Complete)
userStories.description: As a multipart-aware client, I want SSE-S3 to apply across all parts of a multipart upload.
acceptanceCriteria: InitiateMultipartUpload with x-amz-server-side-encryption AES256 generates the DEK once and stores wrapped DEK on the multipart_uploads row (additive sse_key blob column)
UploadPart inherits the upload's DEK and encrypts each part's chunks
CompleteMultipartUpload copies wrapped DEK plus key id from multipart_uploads to objects
GetObject and HEAD echo header and decrypt parts transparently
Unit tests: multipart round-trip with SSE-S3 across 3 parts; plaintext multipart still works
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: SSE-S3 master-key rotation + cmd/strata-rewrap CLI
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to rotate the master key without re-encrypting every chunk; old DEKs stay unwrappable.
acceptanceCriteria: STRATA_SSE_MASTER_KEYS supports comma-separated <keyID>:<hex64> list; first entry is the wrap key, rest are unwrap-only
Each wrapped DEK persists with its keyID; unwrap path looks up the right master
New cmd/strata-rewrap binary walks objects and multipart_uploads, rewraps DEKs to the current master, writes progress to a rewrap_progress table for resumability
Idempotent: re-running on already-current rows is a no-op
Unit tests: PUT under key A, rotate to B, GET still works; new PUT uses B; rewrap CLI converts A to B and verifies
go build ./... passes
go test ./... passes

userStories.title: PutBucketEncryption / GetBucketEncryption / DeleteBucketEncryption + default applied
userStories.description: As a client, I want bucket-default encryption so PUTs without x-amz-server-side-encryption inherit AES256.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET/DELETE /<bucket>?encryption round-trips ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration XML via setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob
PutObject and InitiateMultipartUpload that omit x-amz-server-side-encryption apply the bucket default transparently
DeleteBucketEncryption clears the default; subsequent PUTs without the header store unencrypted
Malformed XML body returns 400 MalformedXML
Unit tests: round-trip XML; default applied on PUT; default cleared
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Notification queue table + hook + filter match
userStories.description: As an event-driven app, I want PutObject/CompleteMultipart/DeleteObject to enqueue a notification event when bucket notification config Filter matches.
acceptanceCriteria: New notify_queue table partitioned by (bucket, hour) with TimeUUID clustering
Server-side hook in PutObject/CompleteMultipart/DeleteObject inserts an Event row when the bucket has notification config
Filter match: NotificationConfiguration's Filter (Prefix/Suffix) honoured; non-matching events are not enqueued
Event payload matches AWS S3 event-message shape (Records[*] with s3.bucket, s3.object, eventName, eventTime)
Unit tests: PUT triggers event; DELETE triggers event; non-matching prefix produces no event
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes

userStories.title: cmd/strata-notify worker + WebhookSink (HMAC)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a worker that drains the notify_queue and POSTs to configured webhook endpoints with an HMAC signature.
acceptanceCriteria: New cmd/strata-notify binary, leader-elected via internal/leader
Polls notify_queue every 5s, fans out to per-target sinks; honours per-target shared secret for HMAC-SHA256 over JSON body
Webhook sink: HTTPS POST with X-Strata-Signature header; non-2xx triggers retry
Exponential backoff: max 6 retries, then row moves to notify_dlq table
Unit tests: webhook sink (httptest); signature verification; retry-then-DLQ; leader handoff
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/notify/... ./cmd/strata-notify/... passes

userStories.title: SQSSink + DLQ inspection
userStories.description: As an AWS-integrating user, I want to deliver events to an SQS queue using AWS SDK credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: SQSSink: AWS SDK SendMessage with the event JSON as MessageBody; queue URL plus region from per-target config
AWS credentials resolved via standard SDK chain (env, IRSA, IAM role)
DLQ inspection endpoint GET /?notify-dlq&bucket=... gated by [iam root] returns paginated dropped events
Unit tests: SQSSink against an AWS SDK mock; DLQ enumeration
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/notify/... passes

userStories.title: Replication queue + hook + status header
userStories.description: As a replication source, when a write matches a configured replication rule, I want the write enqueued and x-amz-replication-status PENDING set.
acceptanceCriteria: New replication_queue table partitioned by (bucket, day) with TimeUUID clustering
PutObject and CompleteMultipart hooks insert one row per object when at least one rule's Filter (Prefix and/or Tag) matches
PutObject response includes x-amz-replication-status PENDING when a rule matches; absent otherwise
PutBucketReplication on an unversioned bucket returns 400 InvalidRequest
Unit tests: matching rule yields PENDING plus queue row; no match yields no header and no row; unversioned bucket rejection
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes

userStories.title: cmd/strata-replicator worker (Strata-to-Strata)
userStories.description: As a DR-conscious operator, I want a worker that drains replication_queue and copies objects to a peer Strata cluster.
acceptanceCriteria: New cmd/strata-replicator binary, leader-elected via internal/leader
Per replication rule, reads source object via meta.Store + data.Backend, PUTs to destination via boto-compatible HTTP against the peer's gateway
Destination endpoint encoded in Destination.AccessControlTranslation.Owner field as <peer-host>:<port>
On success: SET x-amz-replication-status=COMPLETED on source; on 6 retries: SET FAILED and emit replication_lag_seconds metric
Unit tests: rule match yields COMPLETED via httptest peer; destination 5xx yields FAILED after retries; with-tag-filter; with-prefix-filter
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/replication/... ./cmd/strata-replicator/... passes

userStories.title: Server access log buffer table + middleware row writer
userStories.description: As an audit consumer, I need every request logged into a buffer table when source bucket has logging enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: New access_log_buffer table partitioned by (bucket, hour); columns: ts, request_id, principal, source_ip, op, key, status, bytes_sent, object_size, total_time_ms, turn_around_ms, referrer, user_agent, version_id
HTTP middleware in cmd/strata-gateway writes one row per request when meta.Store.GetBucketLogging is set
No row written when logging is not configured for the source bucket
Unit tests: PUT with logging on writes a row; PUT without logging writes no row; row format matches schema
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: cmd/strata-access-log worker (5-min flush, AWS log format)
userStories.description: As an audit consumer, I want buffered log rows assembled into AWS-format log files and written to the configured target bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: New cmd/strata-access-log binary, leader-elected, ticks every 5 minutes (or 5 MiB whichever first)
Reads buffered rows for each source bucket with logging configured, writes one object per flush to <target-bucket>/<target-prefix>YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-SS-<id>

Log line format matches AWS server-access-log: Bucket Owner, Bucket, Time, Remote IP, Requester, Request-ID, Operation, Key, Request-URI, HTTP status, Error Code, Bytes Sent, Object Size, Total Time, Turn-Around Time, Referrer, User-Agent, Version Id
After successful flush, deletes drained rows from the buffer
Unit tests: round-trip via memory backend; format compliance against an AWS sample line
go build ./... passes
go test ./cmd/strata-access-log/... passes

userStories.title: slog migration: structured JSON logs + request_id middleware
userStories.description: As an operator, I want JSON logs with a request_id propagated through every code path.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace standard log calls with log/slog JSON handler in every cmd/ binary and every internal/ package that logs
HTTP middleware reads X-Request-Id or generates a UUID; threads it through context.Context via slog.With('request_id', id)
STRATA_LOG_LEVEL controls level (DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR; default INFO)
Tests: a single request emits logs with the same request_id; missing header gets a generated UUID
make smoke shows JSON log lines per request

userStories.title: slog Cassandra slow-query + RADOS DEBUG hooks

acceptanceCriteria: Cassandra gocql observer logs queries over 100ms at WARN with request_id, table, op, duration
RADOS read/write logs OID and duration at DEBUG with request_id
Threshold configurable via STRATA_CASSANDRA_SLOW_MS (default 100)

userStories.title: Prometheus HTTP request + Cassandra histograms

internal/metrics registers cassandra_query_duration_seconds{table, op} histogram

gocql observer records Cassandra histogram
/metrics endpoint exposes both

gc_queue_depth{region} gauge sampled by GC worker
multipart_active{bucket} gauge
bucket_bytes{bucket, storage_class} gauge sampled hourly
lifecycle_tick_total{action, status} counter
replication_lag_seconds{rule_id} histogram and replication_queue_depth{rule_id} gauge sampled every 30s
notify_delivery_total{sink, status} counter
All workers (gateway/lifecycle/gc/notify/replicator/access-log) expose /metrics

userStories.title: Grafana dashboard JSON
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a pre-wired Grafana dashboard for Strata's metric set.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/grafana/strata-dashboard.json with panels: HTTP latency p50/p95/p99, Cassandra latency by table, RADOS latency by pool, GC depth, multipart active, bucket bytes by class, lifecycle tick rate, replication lag and queue depth, notify delivery rate
deploy/grafana/datasource.yaml provisions Prometheus as default datasource
make up-all brings up a Grafana container with the dashboard auto-loaded
Tests: docker-compose smoke verifies dashboard JSON validates against Grafana API

userStories.title: /healthz + /readyz endpoints
userStories.description: As a k8s probe, I need /healthz (liveness) and /readyz (readiness Cassandra plus RADOS reachable).
acceptanceCriteria: GET /healthz returns 200 plain text 'ok' without dependency checks
GET /readyz queries Cassandra (SELECT 1) and RADOS (stat on a known canary OID) within a 1s timeout; returns 200 only when both succeed
Both endpoints bypass SigV4 middleware regardless of STRATA_AUTH_MODE
deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml ships a healthcheck stanza using /readyz
Tests: kill the gateway's Cassandra session yields /readyz 503; same for RADOS; /healthz always 200

go test ./cmd/strata-gateway/... ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Virtual-hosted-style URL routing
userStories.description: As a modern AWS SDK client, I want bucket.s3.example.com/key to resolve to bucket 'bucket'.
acceptanceCriteria: Router strips the bucket prefix from Host when host matches any pattern in STRATA_VHOST_PATTERN (comma-separated list, first match wins; default '*.s3.local')
Falls back to path-style when host doesn't match any pattern
SigV4 canonicalisation uses the original Host header for signing; bucket extraction is post-signature
Pre-signed URLs work with both styles
Tests: virtual-hosted PUT/GET/HEAD round-trip; path-style still works; signed virtual-hosted matches; mismatched host falls back
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/auth/... passes

userStories.title: audit_log table + state-changing-request middleware hook
userStories.description: As a security auditor, I need every state-changing request appended to an audit_log table with a 30-day TTL.
acceptanceCriteria: New audit_log table partitioned by (bucket, date), columns: event_id timeuuid, ts, principal, action, resource, result, request_id, source_ip; TTL = STRATA_AUDIT_RETENTION (default 30d)
HTTP middleware writes one row per state-changing request after the response is written; reuses request_id from US-015
Read paths (GET/HEAD/list) do not emit audit rows
Tests: every CreateBucket/PutObject/DeleteObject/IAM action emits exactly one row; expired rows disappear after TTL
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes

userStories.title: audit_log read API (?audit, [iam root] gated)
userStories.description: As a security auditor, I want a paginated query API over the audit_log gated to admin principals.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /?audit&start=<ISO8601>&end=<ISO8601>&principal=<id>&bucket=<name>&limit=<n>&continuation=<token> returns paginated JSON
Gated to [iam root] header auth only, no presigned URL support
Filters work in any combination; pagination via opaque continuation token
Tests: filter by each dimension; pagination round-trip; non-admin principal returns 403

userStories.title: objects.part_sizes column + ?partNumber=N GET range serve
userStories.description: As a multipart-aware client, GET /<bucket>/<key>?partNumber=N returns only that part's bytes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add objects.part_sizes list<int> column via additive ALTER
CompleteMultipartUpload populates part_sizes with one entry per part, ordered by PartNumber
GET /<bucket>/<key>?partNumber=N returns the byte range of part N; sets x-amz-mp-parts-count, Content-Length of the part, and the per-part stored x-amz-checksum-<algo>
?partNumber on a non-multipart object returns 400 InvalidArgument
Tests: round-trip a 3-part multipart, fetch each part by number, verify checksum plus size plus ContentLength
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: HEAD ChecksumMode=ENABLED + composite checksum echo
userStories.description: As a checksum-aware client, HEAD with x-amz-checksum-mode ENABLED should return the composite checksum and ChecksumType.
acceptanceCriteria: HEAD with x-amz-checksum-mode ENABLED returns x-amz-checksum-<algo> from object Checksums map and x-amz-checksum-type (COMPOSITE for typical multipart, FULL_OBJECT when client requested it on Initiate)
Without ChecksumMode header, HEAD does not include checksum headers (matches AWS)
GetObject with ChecksumMode behaves the same
Tests: HEAD without mode yields no checksum header; HEAD with mode returns composite; ChecksumType=COMPOSITE for multipart-initiated; FULL_OBJECT path covered

go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: GetObjectAttributes ObjectParts subtree fully populated
userStories.description: As a multipart-aware client, GetObjectAttributes with ObjectParts in x-amz-object-attributes returns per-part sizes plus checksums.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /<bucket>/<key>?attributes with x-amz-object-attributes containing ObjectParts populates the <ObjectParts> subtree with PartsCount, Parts[*].PartNumber, Parts[*].Size, Parts[*].Checksum<algo>
Non-multipart objects return PartsCount=1 with the whole-object size
Tests: 3-part multipart returns 3 entries with correct sizes plus checksums; non-multipart returns PartsCount=1

userStories.title: Versioning null literal: schema + sentinel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a way to address rows written under Versioning=Disabled as the literal version-id 'null'.
acceptanceCriteria: Add objects.is_null bool column via additive ALTER
Sentinel TimeUUID 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 reserved for the 'null' version
meta.Store.PutObject under Versioning=Disabled stores the row with version_id = sentinel and is_null=true
GetObject with versionID='null' resolves to the row with is_null=true
Tests: Disabled-mode PUT and GET ?versionId=null round-trip; query-by-sentinel returns the same row

userStories.title: Versioning null literal: PutObject + ListVersions
userStories.description: As a versioning-aware client, after enabling versioning on a bucket that previously had unversioned writes, the original row is addressable as version 'null'.
acceptanceCriteria: ListObjectVersions surfaces the null-versioned row with VersionId='null' and IsLatest=true if it is current
After SetBucketVersioning(Enabled), the prior null-versioned row remains addressable
PutObject in Enabled mode generates a TimeUUID version (existing behaviour) and does not overwrite a null version unless explicitly requested
Tests: Disabled PUT plus Enabled toggle plus ListObjectVersions includes a 'null' entry; subsequent Enabled PUT adds a TimeUUID version above the null one

userStories.title: Versioning null literal: Suspended PUT/DELETE replaces null atomically
userStories.description: As a versioning-aware client, in Suspended mode, an overwrite or delete removes the prior null-versioned ancestor atomically and writes a new null-versioned row.
acceptanceCriteria: PutObject in Suspended mode: deletes any prior null-versioned row for the key, writes a new one with sentinel and is_null=true
DeleteObject without versionId in Suspended mode: same semantics — null-versioned ancestor removed, delete marker with is_null=true written
Atomic via cassandra LWT ('IF EXISTS' on prior null-versioned row)
Tests: closes test_versioning_obj_plain_null_version_overwrite_suspended, test_versioning_obj_suspend_versions, test_versioning_obj_suspended_copy
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes

userStories.title: Concurrency / race harness

userStories.title: SSE-KMS: aws-kms provider + local-hsm test stub
userStories.description: As an AWS-integrating user, I want a real aws-kms provider; for tests, a deterministic local-hsm stub.
acceptanceCriteria: aws-kms provider: AWS SDK GenerateDataKey plus Decrypt; key id resolved via STRATA_KMS_AWS_REGION plus the request key id
local-hsm provider: deterministic in-process keystore for tests, generates DEK from a hash of (key_id, nonce)
Tests: aws-kms against AWS SDK mock; local-hsm round-trip
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/crypto/kms/... passes

userStories.title: SSE-KMS: PutObject/GetObject wiring
userStories.description: As an enterprise tenant, x-amz-server-side-encryption aws:kms wraps the per-object DEK via KMSProvider.
acceptanceCriteria: PutObject with aws:kms plus x-amz-server-side-encryption-aws-kms-key-id resolves the provider, calls GenerateDataKey, persists wrapped DEK plus key id in objects.sse_key plus sse_key_id (US-004 columns)
GetObject calls UnwrapDEK with the persisted key id; mismatched key id at unwrap time yields 403 AccessDenied
Bucket default encryption (US-007) supports aws:kms with a KMSMasterKeyID — applied transparently when header omitted
Tests: round-trip with vault provider; round-trip with local-hsm; missing key id yields 400; mismatched key id yields 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: cmd/strata-inventory worker (CSV)
userStories.description: As a billing/audit pipeline, I want PutBucketInventoryConfiguration to schedule actual CSV exports of the bucket index.
acceptanceCriteria: New cmd/strata-inventory binary, leader-elected, ticks per inventory configuration (hourly/daily)
Walks objects rows for the source bucket, writes a manifest object (manifest.json) plus a data object (CSV.gz) to the target prefix
PUT /<bucket>?inventory&id=<id> plus GET plus DELETE round-trip InventoryConfiguration XML via setBucketBlob
CSV columns match AWS Inventory: Bucket, Key, VersionId, IsLatest, IsDeleteMarker, Size, LastModifiedDate, ETag, StorageClass, ChecksumAlgorithm
Tests: scheduled tick fires; manifest plus CSV present in target bucket; CSV header matches AWS spec
go build ./... passes
go test ./cmd/strata-inventory/... passes

userStories.title: Access Points: schema + CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As a multi-tenant operator, I want named access points with their own policy and alias.
acceptanceCriteria: New access_points table: name PK, bucket_ref, policy blob, public_access_block blob, network_origin
POST /?Action=CreateAccessPoint plus GetAccessPoint plus DeleteAccessPoint plus ListAccessPoints, AWS-style XML form-encoded (gated by [iam root])
Alias generated as 'ap-<random12>'
Tests: round-trip CRUD; double-create returns AccessPointAlreadyOwnedByYou; delete-missing returns NoSuchAccessPoint
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Access Points: host routing + additive policy
userStories.description: As an access-point client, requests to <alias>.s3-accesspoint.<region>.example.com route to the underlying bucket with the access-point policy applied additively.
acceptanceCriteria: Router accepts <alias>.s3-accesspoint.<region>.<host> hosts; resolves alias to bucket; rewrites the request path to use the bucket name
Access-point policy is evaluated AFTER bucket policy; both must allow the action (intersection semantics)
Network origin restriction (Internet/VPC) honoured; mismatched origin yields 403
Tests: GET via access-point alias reaches underlying bucket; access-point Deny overrides bucket Allow; bucket Deny overrides access-point Allow; mismatched origin yields 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: ScyllaDB CI matrix + docs/backends/scylla.md
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want CI evidence ScyllaDB is a drop-in for Cassandra and a benchmark vs Cassandra.
acceptanceCriteria: New .github/workflows/ci-scylla.yml: weekly schedule plus manual dispatch; runs the existing testcontainers-go integration tests against ScyllaDB 5.x
docs/backends/scylla.md: deployment notes, raft-LWT confirmation, p50/p95/p99 latency table for CreateBucket / SetBucketVersioning / CompleteMultipartUpload (one column Cassandra, one column Scylla)
No code changes required to switch backends
Tests: existing storetest contract passes against ScyllaDB
go build ./... passes
go test -tags integration ./internal/meta/cassandra/... passes against Scylla

userStories.title: Multi-cluster RADOS: config + connection map
userStories.description: As a geo-sep operator, I want RADOS connections per cluster, each with its own keyring and conf.
acceptanceCriteria: STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS=<id>:<conf-path>:<keyring-path>,...
rados.Backend internally holds map[clusterID]*rados.Conn; lazy init on first use; reconnect on connection loss
Existing single-cluster config (STRATA_RADOS_CONF plus STRATA_RADOS_USER plus STRATA_RADOS_KEYRING) maps to cluster id 'default'
Tests: config parsed; missing cluster id fatal at startup; unreachable cluster yields INFO log plus retry on first PutChunks
go build -tags ceph ./... passes
go test -tags ceph ./internal/data/rados/... passes

userStories.title: Multi-cluster RADOS: PutChunks routing + GetChunks dispatch
userStories.description: As a geo-sep operator, I want storage class to drive the cluster choice on writes, and GetChunks to dispatch by ChunkRef.Cluster.
acceptanceCriteria: STRATA_RADOS_CLASSES extends to <CLASS>=<pool>@<cluster>[/<ns>]
PutChunks chooses the cluster from the storage class config
GetChunks dispatches by ChunkRef.Cluster (already on the type)
GC enqueueing carries the cluster id; GC worker dispatches the delete to the right cluster
Tests: lifecycle transition between hot (default) and cold (remote) cluster works end-to-end; cross-cluster GC drains both queues
go build -tags ceph ./... passes
go test -tags ceph ./internal/data/rados/... ./internal/gc/... passes

userStories.title: Online per-bucket shard resize
userStories.description: As an operator of a bucket past tens of millions of objects, I want an online split flow that grows the partition count without downtime.
acceptanceCriteria: strata-admin bucket reshard --bucket <name> --target <N> queues a background rewrite via a reshard_jobs table
Rewrite worker copies rows from old partition layout to new (shard_count = N), LWT-flips bucket.shard_count when done; old rows tombstone-cleaned in a follow-up tick
While shard_count_in_progress flag is set, list-objects reads union of old plus new partition layouts
Idempotent plus resumable from the reshard_jobs progress row
Tests: 1000 objects, reshard 32 to 128, list-objects returns the same set during AND after the resize
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/meta/... passes

userStories.title: cmd/strata-audit-export daily JSON-lines worker
userStories.description: As an operator, I want raw audit rows older than 30d exported to a designated bucket as gzipped JSON-lines and then deleted.
acceptanceCriteria: New cmd/strata-audit-export binary, leader-elected, daily tick
Reads audit_log rows older than STRATA_AUDIT_EXPORT_AFTER (default 30d), writes one gzipped JSON-lines object per day to the configured audit-export bucket / prefix
After successful upload, deletes the source partition for that day
Tests: 100 rows yield tick yield 100 lines in the target bucket yield source partition empty
go build ./... passes
go test ./cmd/strata-audit-export/... passes

userStories.title: examples/ directory for aws-cli/boto3/mc/s3cmd + smoke
userStories.description: As a new Strata user, I want copy-paste examples for common workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: New examples/ directory with subdirectories: aws-cli, boto3, mc, s3cmd
Each subdirectory has a README and runnable scripts covering: bucket setup, multipart upload, presigned URL, lifecycle config, replication setup, SSE-S3 PUT/GET, IAM key rotation
examples/smoke.sh runs every example end-to-end against `make run-memory`
README.md links to examples/
Tests: examples/smoke.sh exits 0 when run against a fresh in-memory gateway
go build ./... passes

userStories.title: Protobuf manifest schema + decoder reads JSON+protobuf
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the manifest blob in protobuf for smaller row size, with backwards-compatible decode.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/data/manifest.proto defines Manifest with the same fields as the current Go struct
Generated Go via buf (vendored output under internal/data/manifestpb/)
Decoder detects format via the first byte (JSON '{' vs protobuf wire-format) and parses accordingly
Tests: round-trip a Manifest as JSON; round-trip as protobuf; decoder reads both
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/data/... passes

userStories.title: Protobuf manifest encoder + background rewriter
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want new objects to serialise the manifest as protobuf, and existing rows opportunistically rewritten.
acceptanceCriteria: Encoder writes protobuf for new objects (toggle: STRATA_MANIFEST_FORMAT=proto|json, default proto)
New cmd/strata-manifest-rewriter binary: leader-elected, walks objects, re-encodes JSON manifests to protobuf, idempotent plus resumable
Tests: PUT new object yields manifest in protobuf; rewriter converts pre-existing JSON row yield protobuf; reader still reads both formats
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/data/... ./cmd/strata-manifest-rewriter/... passes
